data.title: Analysts expect the Altria CEO transition to be seamless
data.description:
Altria (MO) traded slightly higher in Thursday trading after announcing that CEO Billy Gifford will be retiring and current CFO Salvatore Mancuso will succeed him. The CEO switch will be effective on May 14, 2026. In addition, the board elected Heather Newman as CFO.

Goldman Sachs analyst Bonnie Herzog said she viewed the announcement positively on the expectation for a seamless transition and limited disruption of Altria's (MO) ongoing transformation of its vision to Move Beyond Smoking and its 2028 Enterprise Goals, even if the leadership announcement was a little earlier than expected. "Mancuso's appointment as CEO was a result of MO's long-term succession planning process, including the assessment of multiple internal and external candidates," updated Herzog. She added that during Gifford's tenure at Altria (MO) he demonstrated strong leadership capabilities and a thoughtful and strategic approach to managing the business.

Bank of America analyst Lisa Lewandowski noted that Mancuso and Newman are known to investors, and the firm expects the transition to be smooth. "We would not expect any changes in mgmt’s commitment to shareholder returns," advised Lewandowski.

Shares of Altria (MO) were up 1.0% in early afternoon trading to $59.27 vs. the 52-week range of $50.08 to $68.60. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 7.2%.

data.title: Dow climbs and the Nasdaq slides after Oracle’s post-earnings drop
data.description:
Wall Street traded mixed on Thursday as the tech-oriented Nasdaq Composite dragged lower after Oracle (ORCL) posted mixed results, pressuring the AI trade.

The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slid lower by 0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was near even while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) climbed by 1.2%.

Across sectors, nine of the 11 S&P groups were in positive territory, led by Materials. At the other end of the spectrum, Info Tech has been the weakest performer so far Thursday.

Investors also continue to react to Wednesday’s Fed decision to cut its policy rate by 25 basis points to 3.50%-3.75%, which is now its lowest level in three years. 

“The Federal Reserve sent a clear message that the Fed is in the midst of an accommodative phase, largely driven by the recent weakness in the labor market. While 2026 may not bring as many rate cuts as 2025, it's clear that the Fed's prior hiking cycle that disrupted markets and the economy post-Covid is firmly in the rearview mirror, and that's bullish for stocks,” Chris Kampitsis, managing partner of Barnum Financial Group said. 

Furthermore, Deutsche Bank’s Jim Reid stated: “Bond yields have also moved lower, which partly reflects the Fed and the wider risk-off tone this morning.” Reid noted Japan’s robust bond auction and Australia’s unexpected employment contraction as contributing factors. 

Yields were lower across the curve, with the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) falling 3 basis points to 3.51% and the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dropping 4 basis points to 4.11%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Oracle (ORCL) dropped 12.7% while shares of Mosaic (MOS) pushed up by 7.9%.

data.title: Rhythm advances lead asset into late-stage trial in Prader-Willi syndrome
data.description:
Rhythm Pharma (RYTM) added ~15% on Thursday after the company announced plans to advance its lead asset, setmelanotide, into a registrational Phase 3 trial for a rare genetic disorder called Prader-Willi syndrome following a promising mid-stage readout.

Characterized by an extreme sense of hunger (hyperphagia), PWS marks a major unmet medical need affecting an estimated 20,000 Americans and about 40,000 globally, with limited therapeutic options.

Citing initial data from an open-label Phase 2 trial of 18 PWS patients, the Boston, Massachusetts-based biotech said that setmelanotide caused potential therapeutic effects with reductions in BMI and hyperphagia at Months 3 and 6.

Setmelanotide, currently available in the U.S. as Imcivree for several other obesity-related rare disorders, indicated a safety and tolerability profile consistent with prior studies, Rhythm (RYTM) added.

“We are encouraged by these preliminary results, which give us confidence to advance setmelanotide into a registrational Phase 3 trial for PWS,” CEO David Meeker noted.

The company plans to advance setmelanotide into the Phase 3 registrational trial, subject to the successful completion of the ongoing Phase 2 trial, from which additional data are expected in H1 2026.

data.title: Robinhood stock drops after disappointing November trading volumes
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares gapped down 8.3% in Thursday afternoon trading after the company posted markedly lower trading volumes across most asset classes sequentially in November.

The biggest M/M declines were in equity (-37%) and options (-28%) trading. Crypto volumes were also decidedly lower, although events trading swelled last month.

“With equity and crypto markets declining during the month of November, assets under custody declined 6.1% to $325bn, which was the first m/m decline since February," Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Brett Knoblauch, who ranks HOOD as Overweight, wrote in a note to clients.

November was a weaker month for Robinhood (HOOD), though "we believe it was another solid quarter," he added, noting previous months had set new records.

data.title: Mortgage rates edge up this week but remain below the year-to-date average
data.description:
Mortgage rates inched up this week, but remained well below the year-to-date average, the latest Freddie Mac (FMCC) Primary Mortgage Survey showed.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.22% as of December 11, up from 6.19% last week and down from 6.60% in the same period a year ago.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.54%, compared to 5.44% a week ago and 5.84% last year.

"The average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage is well below the year-to-date average of 6.62%, providing some sense of balance to the housing market," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

data.title: Starbucks worker's union stages 'Downtown Throwdown' in Chicago - update
data.description:
Updated 12:11ET with comment from Starbucks spokesperson regarding the SWU strike.

The Starbucks Workers United ((SWU)) strike continues to grind on with the action escalating to a 48-hour demonstration at the company’s locations across Chicago.

The “Downtown Throwdown” will spread to over a dozen restaurants located in Chicago’s Loop and River North, including Chicago’s Reserve Roastery, the world’s largest Starbucks. Demonstrations will run through the day and night until Friday morning in what the union characterizes as “the boldest demonstrations yet of union baristas’ resolve.”

The “Red Cup Rebellion” kicked off mid-November to coincide with the company’s busiest day of the year, spreading to 95 stores in 65 cities within a week. The strike has since included 3,000 baristas across more than 145 stores in 105 cities, making this the longest, nationwide unfair labor practice strike in the company’s history.

The strike has also drawn solidarity from trade unions across the globe as the “No Contract, No Coffee” campaign resulted in demonstrations in South America, Europe and Asia.

In the company’s hometown, three stores currently remain closed due to staffing shortages resulting from the strike, including the iconic Starbucks Reserve Roastery in Seattle’s Capitol Hill neighborhood. While the company states the store has been closed since September as part of a major corporate restructuring and store consolidation, pressure from the union likely contributed to the decision to permanently shutter the restaurant.

To end the action, the union is demanding Starbucks (SBUX) offer employees a new contract that would provide for better hours to improve staffing, higher take-home pay, and a resolution of unfair labor practices by the company.

“Finalizing a fair union contract would cost Starbucks less than one average day’s sales and less than Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol’s $96 million compensation for just four months of work in 2024,” the union said.

Although the SWU says the company has not attempted to negotiate an end to the strike, Starbucks (SBUX) claims the union walked away from talks while also arguing that it already provides top retail employment with an average hourly compensation exceeding $30 in pay and benefits.

Despite attempts to disrupt the business and put a meaningful dent in sales, the strike has so far done seemingly little financial harm to Starbucks (SBUX). This year’s Red Cup Day was the strongest in the company’s history in terms of sales and store traffic., and management maintains that the strikes haven’t significantly disrupted operations nor impacted the company’s bottom-line.

"As we’ve said, 99% of our 17,000 U.S. locations remain open and welcoming customers —including many the union publicly stated would strike but never closed or have since reopened. Regardless of the union’s plans, we do not anticipate any meaningful disruption. When the union is ready to return to the bargaining table, we’re ready to talk," said Starbucks spokesperson Jaci Anderson to Seeking Alpha. 

"Starbucks offers the best job in retail. People choose to work here and stay here—our turnover is less than half the industry average, and we receive more than a million job applications every year," she added.

During the first week of the strike, only 49 coffeehouses experienced any disruption and 29 have already reopened. Additionally, the company stated that less than 1% of all coffeehouses are experiencing any level of disruption from SWU, who represent less than 4% of the company's workforce, the company said.

data.title: DLH signals return to double-digit organic growth amid technology transformation and debt reduction
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: DLH Holdings Corp. (DLHC) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Zachary C. Parker opened by highlighting the company's transformation year, stating that "it has been a time of transformation with both challenges and opportunities," and emphasizing the role of DLH employees in surpassing expectations. He described national award recognition for developing innovative countermeasures for diseases, advancements in naval fleet readiness, and progress in commercial data science and analytics platforms.
  • Parker reported, "We accomplished a great deal this past year and remain on track for enhanced performance going forward," noting DLH's expansion in leading industry organizations and achieving Cybersecurity Maturity Model Level 2 Certification (CMMC). He said this certification positions the company to "compete for higher-value business opportunities within our addressable market, including the C6ISR community."
  • Parker announced a recent contract extension with the VA to provide pharmaceutical and medical logistics services, with the ordering period running through November 2026. He also stated, "Strong cash flow during the quarter resulted in further significant debt reduction of $10.7 million, resulting in a fiscal year-end debt balance of $131.6 million."
  • Parker introduced DLH Cyclone, an AI/ML-powered data science engine, as a "disruptive competitive force challenging traditional norms for large-scale data analytics," noting client interest in transitioning to Cyclone for efficiency and reduced costs. He expressed confidence in returning to "low double-digit organic growth in the future."
  • CFO Kathryn M. Johnbull stated, "We reported revenue of $81.2 million in the fourth quarter versus $96.4 million in the prior year period," citing contract transitions to small business set-asides as the primary reason for the decline. Johnbull added, "EBITDA was $6.6 million for the fourth quarter versus $10.7 million last year."
  • Johnbull highlighted, "We reduced debt by $10.7 million during the quarter, ending the fiscal year with $131.6 million of debt outstanding, a total reduction of $23 million over the 12-month period."

Outlook

  • Management indicated optimism about growth opportunities in fiscal 2026, with Parker stating, "We are confident that with our top talent, our strategic differentiators and best practices, DLH will soon return to low double-digit organic growth in the future."
  • Johnbull projected, "We anticipate fiscal 2026 debt reduction to align with our historical performance of converting approximately 50% to 55% of EBITDA to debt reduction."
  • The company expects to leverage its strong balance sheet and cash flow to pursue a "busy pipeline of opportunities" and make strategic investments in people and programs.

Financial Results

  • DLH reported $81.2 million in revenue for Q4 2025, down from $96.4 million in the prior year period, primarily due to small business set-aside contract conversions and program timing.
  • EBITDA for the quarter was $6.6 million, compared to $10.7 million the previous year, with gross margin pressure from retained investments in innovation resources.
  • Cash generated during the quarter was approximately $10.7 million, supporting the $10.7 million debt reduction. The year-end debt balance stood at $131.6 million, reflecting a $23 million reduction over the fiscal year.
  • For the full year, revenue was $344.5 million, EBITDA was $34 million, and free cash flow was $23 million.

Q&A

  • Joseph Gomes, NOBLE Capital Markets: Asked about the Head Start program protest. President Parker replied, "No, we were not a participant in the protest effort," and explained the administration's focus on small business set-asides led to DLH not participating.
  • Gomes questioned the status of CMOP awards and DLH's role. Parker responded that DLH withdrew joint venture bids but supported a small business partner, with some decisions pending. Johnbull added, "There are 3 locations remaining, which we will continue to operate as the VA executes their strategy."
  • Gomes inquired about a return to historical gross and EBITDA margins. Parker outlined that new business within the VA and awards in digital transformation and cybersecurity are expected to drive margin recovery in "calendar Q1," with optimism for both defense and nondefense sectors. Johnbull reaffirmed, "Growth is the imperative and really why we've worked hard to diversify the base of customers and capabilities."
  • Gomes asked about the pipeline size. Parker stated, "We were north of $3 billion" in qualified opportunities over a 24-plus month period.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on transition risks, lost contracts, and margin recovery, with a neutral to slightly negative tone as they pressed for specifics on pipeline, contract prospects, and timing of margin recovery.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, emphasizing strategy, innovation, and growth prospects. Phrases such as "We are confident" and "we feel really still very optimistic" signaled strong conviction.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst tone remained consistent, though management appeared more assertive and detailed about new products and the timeline for margin recovery.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured a more explicit discussion of technological products (DLH Cyclone) and the impact of contract transitions. Compared to Q3, there was a clearer articulation of a return to double-digit organic growth and a more forward-looking focus on debt reduction targets and margin recovery timing.
  • Management's confidence level was higher, with specific references to innovation labs and strategic differentiators. Analyst skepticism about contract transitions and margin restoration persisted, mirroring last quarter's concerns.
  • Metric changes included a decrease in revenue and EBITDA, but a larger debt reduction and increased emphasis on technology-driven growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Contract transitions to small business set-asides continue to reduce revenue, with management citing the Head Start and CMOP programs as recent examples.
  • Management identified the risk of timing in new contract awards, stating that many expected RFPs and awards had been delayed but are "now starting to hit."
  • Pressure on margins remains due to retained investments in innovation resources even as revenue declines, with management relying on pipeline conversion and new business wins to restore profitability.

Final Takeaway

DLH Holdings Corp. closed fiscal 2025 by emphasizing its strategic transformation into a technology-driven solutions provider, ongoing investment in proprietary platforms like Cyclone, and meaningful debt reduction ahead of schedule. Management projects a return to double-digit organic growth, underpinned by a robust $3 billion pipeline and enhanced capabilities in digital transformation and cybersecurity, while acknowledging the near-term challenges posed by contract transitions and margin pressure. The company remains confident in its ability to deliver stakeholder value as it enters fiscal 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nike extends partnership with LSU, launches NIL program with 10 LSU athletes
data.description:
Nike (NKE) is extending its five-decade partnership with Louisiana State University ((LSU)) sports, outfitting the college’s athletes with uniforms and gear until at least 2036.

The company also announced the launch of its “Blue Ribbon Elite” Name, Image, and Likeness program at LSU -- known more commonly as NIL -- representing 10 athletes from various LSU sports programs.

“The athlete-centered approach takes shape for the first time at LSU through the addition of 10 top Tigers athletes to Nike’s NIL roster, illustrating that for Nike, it’s not just about outfitting the best – it’s about creating with the boldest,” Nike said in a statement.

Through its NIL program, these 10 athletes representing LSU football, baseball, basketball, volleyball, and gymnastics, will collaborate with Nike (NKE) on product development and content creation, and participate in Nike (NKE) brand campaigns similar to Alabama wide receiver Ryan Williams’ DICK’s Sporting Goods (DKS) campaign and USC and UCLA athletes featured in NikeSKIMS ads.

The NIL program “is about more than gear,” said Nike’s head of Global Sports Marketing. “It’s about collaboration, creativity, and meaningful impact, giving athletes a platform to influence product, innovation, storytelling and culture.”

Nike shares are up more than 1% on Thursday, flirting with resistance at its 200-day moving average.

data.title: Ciena outlines $5.7B-$6.1B fiscal 2026 revenue target with 24% growth driven by AI demand
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Ciena Corporation (CIEN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Gary Smith announced that Ciena reported record fiscal fourth quarter and full year revenue of $1.35 billion and $4.77 billion, respectively, attributing this to “sustained, purposeful investment and focus on leading high-speed connectivity technologies, together with disciplined execution and deep collaboration with our customers.” He highlighted Q4 earnings per share of $0.91, a 69% year-over-year increase, and a full-year EPS of $2.64, up 45% from the prior year. Smith also noted record orders of $7.8 billion for the year, leading to a record backlog entering 2026.

  • Smith emphasized accelerating demand from cloud customer providers, including large hyperscalers and emerging neoscalers, stating, “Orders from cloud providers are very strong, and ramping across our portfolio and they constitute a substantial portion of our growing backlog.” He mentioned that service provider orders were up nearly 70% for the year and that the top 3 service providers’ revenue grew 16% from 2024 to 2025. Smith reported that Ciena’s optical market share increased by 2 points year-to-date, and he expects further gains in 2026.

  • Smith described strategic wins including a large MOFN project in India, multiple major MOFN wins in new geographies, and a threefold growth in “in and around the data center opportunities” from 2024 to 2025. He highlighted surpassing the target for pluggable revenue, reaching more than $168 million in FY 2025, and initial shipments of WaveLogic 6 Nano 800-gig pluggables.

  • On acquisitions, Smith noted the completion of the Nubis Communications purchase, stating that “Nubis’ technologies and expertise will help us address the scale-up and scale-out opportunities inside the data center.”

  • CFO Marc Graff stated, “Our Q4 gross margin sequentially improved and exceeded [indiscernible] our guide by 90 basis points. This was largely due to higher revenue and software mix.” Graff also reported, “We left the year with $1.4 billion in cash after generating $371 million in cash from operations in Q4 and free cash flow of $326 million.”

Outlook

  • Ciena updated its fiscal 2026 revenue guidance to a range of $5.7 billion to $6.1 billion, reflecting nearly 24% annual growth at the midpoint versus the 17% growth rate previously discussed in September.

  • Graff stated, “We continue to expect gross margins for fiscal '26 to be in the range of 43%, plus or minus 1 point.” He further noted, “We expect adjusted operating expense in fiscal '26 to be flat at approximately $1.52 billion after accounting for the Nubis operating expenses post acquisition.”

  • Ciena now anticipates fiscal 2026 operating margins to improve to 17%, plus or minus 1 point, with capital expenditures projected between $250 million and $275 million.

  • For Q1 2026, revenue is expected in the range of $1.35 billion to $1.43 billion, adjusted gross margin between 43% and 44%, and adjusted operating expenses of approximately $380 million.

Financial Results

  • Q4 revenue was $1.35 billion, up 20% year-over-year, and $70 million above the midpoint of guidance. Full-year revenue reached $4.77 billion, up 19% year-over-year.

  • The Optical business grew 19% year-over-year, with RLS revenue up 72%. The Routing and Switching business increased 49% year-over-year, and Global Services grew 25%. Blue Planet achieved $34 million in revenue in Q4 and $115 million for the year, achieving full-year profitability.

  • Adjusted gross margin for Q4 was 43.4%; for the year, it was 42.7%. Q4 adjusted operating expense was approximately $409 million and $1.51 billion for the year. Q4 adjusted operating margin was 13.2%, operating margin for the year reached 11.2%. Annual adjusted EPS was $2.64.

  • Ciena exited the year with about $5 billion of backlog, with $3.8 billion in hardware and software.

Q&A

  • Ruben Roy, Stifel: Asked how new scale across and DCOM opportunities are reflected in guidance and for details on hyperscaler wins. Marc Graff: Confirmed that all new opportunities are included in the guidance and that the “in and around data center” revenue portion is increasing to low double digits in 2026’s guide. Gary Smith: Explained, “We’ve got 3 out of the 4 hyperscalers have selected us for their scale across training models…they are clearly hundreds of millions each but they are different in terms of their scale at this stage.”

  • Simon Leopold, Raymond James: Inquired about the timeline for new hyperscaler projects and customer breakdown. Gary Smith: Stated, “I would expect us to take revenue for all 3 of these hyperscalers in '26 or begin to take revenue in '26… the ramp on this as we get to '27 and through '28.” Marc Graff: Specified AT&T as one of the three 10% revenue customers in Q4.

  • Atif Malik, Citi: Asked about Nubis adoption and gross margin drivers. Gary Smith: Noted “accelerated interest now that they’re part of a broader company.” Scott McFeely: Clarified Nubis’ portfolio opportunities start in ’26 and ’27. Marc Graff: Identified ramping 800-gig pluggables and customer pricing discussions as margin drivers.

  • George Notter, Wolfe Research: Sought clarity on supply chain constraints. Marc Graff: Highlighted constraints mainly in photonics and optical parts, with increased CapEx to support demand. Scott McFeely: Added that Ciena’s early view and vertical integration have been advantages.

  • Tal Liani, BofA: Questioned the visibility into growth and margin cyclicality. Marc Graff: Asserted high near-term visibility, stating, “We’re essentially sold out… we’ve got really good visibility of what the next several quarters look like just because we’ve got those orders in place.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on growth visibility, hyperscaler wins, scale across timing, supply constraints, and margin sustainability, signaling a mix of curiosity and skepticism, particularly about the durability of demand and margin expansion.

  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, using phrases such as “absolute conviction” and “increasing confidence that the durability of demand and our business and financial trajectory are very strong.” During Q&A, management responded directly, provided granular detail, and acknowledged headwinds, but continued to express optimism.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone shifted from cautious optimism to robust confidence, while analysts increased their focus on forward visibility and margin drivers.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Fiscal 2026 revenue growth guidance increased from 17% to nearly 24% at the midpoint, a notable revision from last quarter.

  • Management’s confidence has visibly strengthened, with explicit references to record orders, accelerating demand, and market share gains.

  • Analysts’ questions shifted from general demand and portfolio strategy last quarter to seeking detail on specific hyperscaler wins, timing, and margin drivers in the current call.

  • Key metrics such as revenue, EPS, backlog, and operating margin all showed sequential and year-over-year improvement.

  • Strategic focus has intensified on AI-driven opportunities, cloud provider wins, and rapid scaling of “in and around data center” solutions, with increased capital allocation to support these priorities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged supply chain constraints, particularly in photonics and optical components, and detailed proactive capacity expansions and supplier negotiations as mitigations.

  • Rising input costs and ramping new product introductions were cited as headwinds to gross margin, with expectations for improvement as scale and pricing actions take effect in late fiscal 2026.

  • Analysts noted potential risks in supply chain, margin cyclicality, and the timing of large hyperscaler projects.

Final Takeaway

Ciena’s fourth quarter call highlighted record revenue, robust EPS growth, and an unprecedented order backlog, underpinned by accelerating AI-driven demand from both cloud and service provider customers. The company raised its fiscal 2026 revenue growth target to nearly 24% and expects further operating margin expansion, supported by major wins with hyperscalers, expanded data center solutions, and ongoing strategic investments. Management projects continued market leadership and durable demand, while actively addressing supply chain and margin risks to capitalize on the massive network investments required for the AI era.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Paramount is now the frontrunner to buy Warner Bros. on prediction market Polymarket
data.description:
According to prediction market Polymarket, Paramount Skydance (PSKY) now has higher odds of acquiring Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) than Netflix (NFLX).

A high-stakes battle between Paramount (PSKY) and Netflix (NFLX) over Warner (WBD) is set to unfold after the David Ellison-led firm launched a hostile bid for the entire company on Monday. That move came after Netflix (NFLX) last week said it would buy Warner's (WBD) studio and streaming assets in a deal with an enterprise value of $82.7B.

See Polymarket's update below:

Paramount (PSKY) top boss Ellison on Wednesday sent a lengthy letter to Warner's (WBD) shareholders, trying to convince them that his $108B total buyout proposal was better than Netflix's (NFLX) offer. 

Bloomberg News on Wednesday said the bidding war between PSKY and NFLX would stretch into next year, citing people familiar with WBD's thinking.

According to rival prediction market Kalshi, the odds of Paramount (PSKY) buying Warner (WBD) before December 2026 are at 51%.
